title: R5 district (flat-roof envelope) - maximum base height and maximum building height
description: DRAFT extraction (needs_review) of the flat-roof envelope HEIGHT constraints for the exact R5 district (no letter suffix) per NYC Zoning Resolution section 23-422 (City of Yes for Housing Opportunity, Last Amended 2024-12-05). Two SEPARATE typed height constraints are emitted: maximum base height (35 ft) and maximum building height (45 ft). The source states NO minimum base height (a documented limitation, not a zero). These heights are street-width-INDEPENDENT. The required setback ABOVE the 35 ft base is a SEPARATE constraint (rule r5-setback, section 23-423) whose depth is street-width conditional. R5 is encoded SEPARATELY from R5A/R5B/R5D - no dimension is inherited. NOT a Verified determination: a candidate representation awaiting raw-HTML source verification and G6 qualified-human legal approval. effective_from is the section's Last Amended date; evaluating as_of before it yields a visible not-effective outcome. When a commercial overlay, special district, historic district, or large-site context is present, the citywide value is NOT emitted as final: coverage downgrades to professional_review_required.

citations.quote: In the district indicated, except R5 Districts with a letter suffix, the maximum base height shall be 35 feet, and the maximum building height shall be 45 feet. At a height not higher than the maximum base height, a setback shall be provided in accordance with Section 23-423.

inputs.name: zoning_district
inputs.description: Residence district governing the lot. District scope is enforced by applicability (equals R5 via in_set), NOT an input enum: a non-R5 district is a visible not_applicable. A letter-suffixed variant (R5A/R5B/R5D) is deliberately NOT matched here - each is encoded in its own rule.

inputs.name: overlay_present
inputs.description: True iff the canonical profile's zoning.commercial_overlays[] is non-empty. A present overlay may modify residence-district height per section 23-44; when true the citywide base value is not emitted as final (professional_review_required).

inputs.name: special_district_present
inputs.description: True iff the canonical profile's zoning.special_districts[] is non-empty. A mapped special district may modify height per section 23-44; when true -> professional_review_required.

inputs.name: historic_district
inputs.description: True iff the lot is in a designated Historic District (section 23-426 base-height match). No canonical property_profile field currently exposes this; when a caller supplies true -> professional_review_required. When unknown/absent the historic-district override is a documented limitation, not silently cleared.

inputs.name: large_site
inputs.description: True iff a large-site context (section 23-425) applies. No canonical field currently exposes this; when a caller supplies true -> professional_review_required.

outputs.name: max_base_height
outputs.description: Maximum base height (ft) - the maximum height of the street wall before the required setback. Separate typed constraint; not a buildable-envelope result.

outputs.name: max_building_height
outputs.description: Maximum building height (ft). Separate typed constraint; not a buildable-envelope result.

parameters.name: base_height_ft
parameters.note: R5 maximum base height 35 ft (section 23-422). No minimum base height is stated in the source. Candidate value pending raw-HTML verification and G6 approval.

parameters.name: building_height_ft
parameters.note: R5 maximum building height 45 ft (section 23-422). Candidate value pending raw-HTML verification and G6 approval.

steps.note: Maximum base height for R5 (section 23-422 table).
Maximum building height for R5 (section 23-422 table).

exceptions.description: Section 23-422 states no MINIMUM base height for R5; only the maximum (35 ft) is encoded. The absence of a stated minimum is a documented limitation, never represented as a zero minimum.
A commercial overlay may modify residence-district height (section 23-44); the citywide base value is not emitted as final. Professional review required.
A mapped special district may modify height (section 23-44); the citywide base value is not emitted as final. Professional review required.
In a designated Historic District the base height may be required to match adjacent buildings (section 23-426); the citywide base value is not emitted as final. Professional review required.
A large-site context (section 23-425) may govern; the citywide base value is not emitted as final. Professional review required.

special_district_interactions.description: Special Purpose Districts and commercial overlays (section 23-44 and Articles VIII-XIV) may modify residence-district height. When present, their provisions govern and must be evaluated by the corresponding special rule; this general rule does not apply the modification and downgrades to professional_review_required via the overlay/special-district exceptions.

uncertainty_policy.note: An uncertain, split, sliver, or invalid M2-T013 geometric district assignment is never collapsed into a single definitive district: coverage downgrades to professional_review_required (or data_conflict) and the geometric uncertainty is recorded in the trace.

limitations: Draft extraction pending raw-HTML source verification and G6 qualified-human legal approval; not a Verified determination.
No minimum base height is stated in section 23-422 for R5; only maximum caps are encoded.
The required setback above the 35 ft base (section 23-423) is a SEPARATE constraint (rule r5-setback) and is street-width conditional; it is not part of this height rule.

The higher section 23-424 qualifying-residential-site envelope (base 45 ft / building 55 ft) is a SEPARATE rule (r5-qrs-height) and applies only where the section 12-10 qualifying-residential-site test is met; that geography cannot be evaluated from the canonical profile.

Historic-district (23-426) and large-site (23-425) overrides have no canonical property_profile field; they are surfaced as professional_review_required only when a caller supplies the flag, and are otherwise a documented limitation.
